title: Cell cycle
description: Test your knowledge of the cell cycle!

assessment_items.question_content:
The following graph shows how cellular DNA content changes over the course of a typical mitotic cell cycle in rapidly proliferating human cells.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the graph, which of the following labeled phases corresponds to S phase of the cell cycle?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph with the x-axis labeled time, hours with intervals at 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25, and the y axis labeled DNA content, picograms with intervals increasing and marked at 6 and 12. The graph has 4 regions, labeled A, B, C, and D. Region A is a straight line at 6 on the y axis and extends from 0 to 11 on the x axis. Region B is a rising diagonal line that begins at 11 on the x axis and 6 on the y axis and ends at 19 on the x axis and 12 on the y axis. Region C is a straight line at 12 on the y axis and extends from 18 on the x axis to 23 on the x axis. Region D begins at 12 on the y axis and 23 on the x axis. At 24 on the x axis the line drops straight down to 6 on the x-axis.

choices.content: Phase A, because it is the longest phase of the cell cycle.
Phase B, because the amount of cellular DNA is doubled during this phase.
Phase C, because it occurs immediately after the amount of cellular DNA is doubled.
Phase D, because the amount of cellular DNA is halved during this phase.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following graph shows how cellular DNA content changes over the course of a typical mitotic cell cycle in rapidly proliferating human cells.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on the graph, which of the following statements is correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph with the x-axis labeled time, hours with intervals at 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25, and the y axis labeled DNA content, picograms with intervals increasing and marked at 6 and 12. The graph has 4 regions, labeled G1, S, G2, and M. Region G1 is a straight line at 6 on the y axis and extends from 0 to 11 on the x axis. Region S is a rising diagonal line that begins at 11 on the x axis and 6 on the y axis and ends at 19 on the x axis and 12 on the y axis. Region G2 is a straight line at 12 on the y axis and extends from 18 on the x axis to 23 on the x axis. Region M begins at 12 on the y axis and 23 on the x axis. At 24 on the x axis the line drops straight down to 6 on the x-axis.

choices.content: Human cells spend the majority of the cell cycle in interphase.
The amount of cellular DNA remains constant throughout the cell cycle.
Human cells spend the same amount of time in M phase as they do in interphase.
The amount of cellular DNA is halved during interphase.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student is examining an onion root tip cell under a microscope. Based on her observations, the student proposes that the onion root tip cell has completed interphase and is now in the first phase of mitosis. 

**Which of the following pieces of evidence would best support the student’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Discrete chromosomes are scattered throughout the cell’s nucleus.
The cell’s DNA is dispersed within the nucleus as chromatin.
The cell’s chromosomes are aligned across the metaphase plate.
Separated sister chromatids are being pulled towards opposite ends of the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student is examining a whitefish blastula cell under a microscope. Based on his observations, the student proposes that the whitefish blastula cell is in interphase. 

**Which of the following pieces of evidence would best support the student’s claim?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The genetic content of the cell exists as dispersed chromatin.
The mitotic spindle is fully assembled, with its poles at opposite ends of the cell.
A cleavage furrow has formed on the cell’s surface.
Sister chromatids have separated and are moving toward opposite poles of the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
A partial hepatectomy, or the surgical removal of a section of the liver, is often performed to treat tumors or other abnormal growths in the liver. Following a partial hepatectomy, resting liver cells re-enter the cell cycle, duplicating until the excised portion of the liver has been regenerated.

**According to the information above, performing a partial hepatectomy causes liver cells to**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: exit G$_0$ phase and enter G$_1$ phase
exit G$_1$ phase and enter G$_0$ phase
exit mitosis and enter G$_1$ phase
exit meiosis and enter G$_1$ phase

assessment_items.question_content:
In response to critically low nutrient levels, yeast cells often enter into a dormant, non-dividing state. This state allows yeast cells to survive until nutrient levels are restored, at which point the cells typically re-enter the cell cycle.

**According to the information above, critically low nutrient levels cause yeast cells to**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: transition from G$_1$ to G$_0$ phase
transition from G$_0$ to G$_1$ phase
transition into mitosis
transition into meiosis

assessment_items.question_content:
**A mouse skin cell containing $40$ chromosomes undergoes mitosis. This process results in the formation of**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: two daughter nuclei, each with $40$ chromosomes
two daughter nuclei, each with $20$ chromosomes
four daughter nuclei, each with $40$ chromosomes
four daughter nuclei, each with $20$ chromosomes

assessment_items.question_content:
The image below depicts a somatic cell nucleus before DNA replication:


[[☃ image 1]]

**If the cell undergoes mitosis, which of the following correctly depicts the daughter nuclei that would result?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An image of a cell with 4 identical chromatids.

choices.content: ![]Two daughter cells, each containing four identical chromosomes.(https://cdn.kastatic.org/ka-perseus-images/f486e168c97482fbc32da1133c96007f80f580b4.svg)
![Two daughter cells, each containing two identical chromosomes.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a38b0face86abb14ffa2fb3a55630601.svg)
![Four daughter cells, each containing four identical chromosomes.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ee50d9d33802134c3baeddd1ed0b571.svg)
![Four daughter cells, each containing two identical chromosomes.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6fbd987eda21bf95ff5f90999c4abbdd.svg)
